reason: Part A §2.25 verbatim: Razorpay PA entitled to recover card-association penalties by deducting from settlement amount; may also initiate recovery proceedings. Direct answer.
Definitions: Refund verbatim defines the returned amount (Transaction Amount or part thereof) and destination (the original Payment Instrument). Direct answer.
COMPLIANCE WITH PAYMENT AGGREGATOR GUIDELINES lines 524-538 verbatim names https://razorpay.com/grievances/ as the breach-report destination. Direct answer.
Part A §3.2 verbatim: statutory variations in applicable taxes are borne by the merchant. Direct answer to the GST-rate-increase question.

Part A §1.2 verbatim: royalty-free, irrevocable, sub-licensable license already granted; 'Razorpay PA may use Your materials in its marketing and promotional materials without requiring any incremental consent from You.' Direct.

Part IA lines 673-716 verbatim: 'not less than 3 days' return deadline on rental-Device termination. Direct answer. Note: near-duplicate of A-009 on the same clause/number — diversity waste but both pass individually.

Part A §16.2 verbatim: termination 'at any time for any reason (including, without limitation, for any activity that may create harm or loss to the goodwill of Razorpay PA)'. Direct no-reason-needed answer.
Part A §7.2 verbatim: aggregate liability cap equals one (1) month fees paid for the specific Service, proportionally reduced for merchant-contributed loss. Direct.
Part IA lines 673-716 verbatim: 'not less than 3 days' rental Device return. Same clause as A-006; near-duplicate slot but the row itself passes PS criteria.
Part B Part I §7.1 verbatim: 'Razorpay PA's role will strictly be that of a SaaS provider and will not be that of a payment service provider.' Direct.
Part A §12.4 verbatim prohibits merchant storage of full card credentials including CVV 'under any circumstance'. Directly resolves the caching-CVV question as a no.
Part A §2.21 verbatim covers both legs: prior-written-consent requirement for third-party orchestrators AND the immediate suspend/terminate remedy for breach. Direct.
Part A §2.8 verbatim: failure to submit requisitioned KYC entitles Razorpay PA to suspend Services and/or stop settlement until documents are submitted to sole satisfaction. Direct.

Definitions: Chargeback verbatim covers initiator (Facility Provider on request from its customer), trigger (debit reversal following examination), and financial consequence (Razorpay PA charged the amount plus fines/penalties). Direct.

Part B Part II §1 verbatim: 'Razorpay shall not be liable to provide any Services under these Terms until the Sponsor Bank has issued a registration in Your favour' and onboarding is a prerequisite for customer E-Mandate Registration Requests. Direct on both legs.

Axis 'settlement_action_type' genuinely forks Usage-Data sharing consent (§2.27, no per-instance revocation) vs. §1.10 excess/erroneous settlement clawback (sole discretion). Two distinct operational regimes; question is generic enough that neither axis value is pre-revealed.

Axis 'service_context_triggering_indemnity' forks Sub-Merchant indemnity (PP §§1.14-1.18, applies only to PA/marketplace merchants with Sub-Merchants) vs. TokenHQ breach indemnity (Part III). Mutually exclusive service contexts; load-bearing for indemnity scope and supporting-obligation differences.

Axis 'service_scope' forks aggregation-active (§1.7 Escrow-credit prerequisite) vs. device-only (Part IA 1.2 explicit 'shall not be liable for settlement of the funds when only providing Devices'). Directly-contractualized liability split.

Axis 'compliance_obligation_source' forks §2.6 RBI/PMLA/KYC vs. §9.2 Card Payment Network Rules — but both bind every merchant simultaneously and neither carves out the other. A direct Cat A answer listing obligations under both clauses resolves the question. Same 'concurrent obligations presented as alternatives' defect class as v2 B-007 and v3 B-015.

Axis 'data_sharing_consent_type' forks §2.27 Usage-Data consent (no revocation path in text) vs. GENERAL-clause Affiliate/Facility-Provider info-sharing consent (explicit razorpay.com/support revocation). The ToS text itself treats the two consents asymmetrically; materially different operational outcome.

Axis 'recovery_source_type' forks §1.10 excess/erroneous recovery (sole discretion, no ordering) vs. GENERAL-clause scheduled deduction ordering ('first deduct its fees and other liabilities... followed by other deductions, based on chronological order of instructions'). Borderline because both could apply to same account, but the ordering/discretion rules differ materially and the axis is not pre-revealed.

Axis 'recovery_trigger_type' forks E-Mandate Sponsor-Bank/NPCI/governmental fraud penalty recovery (Definitions:: 850-887, obligation to provide transaction info forthwith) vs. early-settlement 3-working-day Escrow clawback (Definitions:: 834-848, 'absolute right to recover'). Two distinct triggers, distinct merchant duties; question text is generic.

Axis 'service_type' forks Offline-PA Device inspection ('during business hours, with or without prior notice') vs. TokenHQ compliance audit ('at any time upon notice'). Different notice regimes tied to fundamentally different audit types (physical device vs. compliance logs).

Axis 'suspension_trigger_type' forks §2.2 inaccurate-existing-info → immediate suspension on reasonable grounds (cure by correcting info) vs. §2.8 non-submission of requisitioned docs → suspend until submitted to sole satisfaction. Reinstatement paths materially differ; axis not pre-revealed.

Axis 'compliance_demand_type' forks §2.6 RBI/PMLA/KYC-source demand vs. §14.9 Facility-Provider/Card-Network technical directive. Different drivers, different merchant actions; load-bearing on 'who is driving'. Borderline with B-004 but distinguishable because §2.6 and §14.9 address different obligation types (info vs. system-upgrade) rather than being two simultaneously-binding compliance frameworks.

Axis 'service_type' forks Subscription Services ('records of its activities under these terms' = broad) vs. TokenHQ ('log of all instances of obtaining customer consent' = narrow). Borderline because Subscription typically triggers TokenHQ concurrently, but the per-clause scope of record-keeping is genuinely different at the text level.

Axis 'recovery_trigger_type' forks §1.10 Razorpay PA excess/erroneous recovery vs. PA-Guidelines §6 Facility Provider direct-claim rights ('Facility Provider may: hold funds and/or make a direct claim for recovery'). Distinct recovering parties and distinct merchant counterparties for resolution.

Axis 'audit_compliance_domain' forks Subscription/TokenHQ audit (Razorpay + Facility Providers, upon notice) vs. PA-Guidelines security audit (Razorpay PA only, 'sole satisfaction'). Borderline concurrency issue but text-level audit-rights holder genuinely differs by domain.

Axis 'recovery_service_context' forks Offline-PA Device fee shortfall → e-NACH/e-mandate fallback vs. E-Mandate insufficient-held-funds → debit-note fallback. Two distinct fallback mechanisms tied to service context; operationally different merchant preparation.

Axis 'nature_of_settlement_deduction' forks §1(e) insufficient Fee Credits → direct Transaction-Amount fee deduction (recourse: purchase more Fee Credits) vs. §3.4 reconciliation discrepancy → 3-day reporting deadline with liability waiver if missed. Different triggers and different merchant remedies; unrelated to v3 B-015's PCI-obligation failure.

PA-Guidelines §6 'promptly report security incidents' uses purely qualitative timing and defers to undefined 'Applicable Laws' for the numeric window. Names known (the Razorpay grievance URL obligation), missing (numeric SLA), escalation (dashboard ticket + external counsel on RBI/CERT-In/DPDP).

Part A §2.18 'forthwith upon request' for audit record production — vague-language with no numeric window, no format spec, no urgency-tier. Names known/missing/escalation (dashboard ticket + §18 written notice + external counsel on PMLA/IT-Act statutory deadlines).

Part B Part IB §1.2 conditions 25-day PA-CB Outward on risk/compliance check but gives no hold-duration cap; external deferral to PA Guidelines which also silent on merchant-level hold ceiling. Full what-known/what-missing/escalation structure.

Clause 14.5 (via gap: lines 273-273) bars Razorpay from being made a party to product disputes but ToS is silent on what happens when regulator independently names Razorpay. Full escalation path named (dashboard + RBI Ombudsman Scheme + counsel).

Same PA-Guidelines §6 'promptly report' ambiguity as C-001, framed around regulatory-window conflict (CERT-In 6h vs. DPDP 72h vs. unspecified contractual). Vague-language + external deferral; escalation named. Near-duplicate of C-001 (see diversity note) but passes individually.

Part A §2.1 Secure-Credentials blanket merchant liability — silent on interaction with RBI PA-license fraud-monitoring/grievance obligations. Full known/missing/escalation (dashboard ticket + §18 + external counsel on RBI PA circular).

§2.10 'forthwith inform in writing' on PEP status — vague-language on both timing and channel (no hours/days, no dashboard-vs-email-vs-physical spec). Proper what-known/what-missing; escalation names multiple channels plus external counsel.

Part IA SMS-Pay CNP clause puts 'explicit consent' on merchant but ToS defines nothing about format, documentation, or retention. External deferral to TRAI TCCCPR / DPDP named; escalation via dashboard + counsel.

SNRR §8.6 'specific/incidental to the business' — vague-language scope undefined for platform/gateway fees; external deferral to RBI SNRR Master Direction (2016). Escalation via authorised-dealer bank + dashboard + tax counsel.

Part B Part I §4.2 defers post-settlement fraud disputes to two named RBI circulars but is silent on merchant-facing claw-back procedure (notice/cure/dispute window). Both named circulars cited; escalation path specific.

Part A §9.3 imposes 'forthwith reimburse' card-network fines and suspend/terminate remedy but is entirely silent on notice period, documentation sharing, or merchant dispute mechanism. Proper what-missing + escalation (dashboard + addendum negotiation + counsel).

Part A §2.13 'credits that can be monetized/re-sold/converted' — vague-language threshold undefined for loyalty rewards; deferral to RBI PPI framework (Master Direction on PPIs 2021 named). Specific escalation.

Part B Part I §6.1 requires PA-Guidelines compliance 'during the entire term' but ToS is silent on implementation grace period or Razorpay notice duty for mid-contract RBI amendments. Known/missing/escalation (dashboard + counsel on PA Guidelines amendments).

Part A §3.5 conditions reduced withholding on LTDC availability but is silent on mid-cycle lapse mechanics (rate snap-back timing, notification duty). Deferral to Income Tax Act; escalation via Tax/TDS dashboard ticket + tax counsel.

USAGE lines 148-154 §2.23 end-user blacklist 'reasonable discretion' with explicit 'not obligated to provide prior notice or reasons' — vague-language on both threshold and reassessment frequency. Escalation via dashboard + §18 notice + counsel on reasonableness under Indian contract law.
